prompt: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
Which animal has the largest heart of any creature that has ever lived?
What force is mainly responsible for causing ocean tides?
The Great Barrier Reef — the world's largest coral reef system — lies off the coast of which country?
An octopus has three hearts. What does each of the two 'gill hearts' actually do?
options: Pump blood through the gills to pick up oxygen

prompt: The Pacific Ocean is so vast it covers roughly what fraction of Earth's total surface area?
When do 'spring tides' — the most extreme high and low tides — occur?
options: When the Sun, Earth, and Moon are aligned
When the Moon is at its farthest from Earth

prompt: About 75% of deep-sea animals produce their own light. What is this ability called?
Hydrothermal vents on the ocean floor spew superheated water. How hot can the water ejected by a 'black smoker' vent get?
options: Up to 1,000 °C — near the melting point of rock
Up to 100 °C — boiling point at sea level
Up to 60 °C — like a hot bath
Up to 400 °C — well above boiling, kept liquid by pressure

prompt: The deepest known point on Earth, Challenger Deep, sits at roughly what depth?
A female anglerfish dangles a glowing lure to attract prey in the deep sea. What actually produces that light?
options: A chemical reaction triggered when she opens her mouth
A specialized electric organ in her forehead
Concentrated deep-sea phosphorus she absorbs through diet
Symbiotic bioluminescent bacteria living inside the lure

prompt: Octopus blood is blue instead of red. Which copper-based protein is responsible for carrying oxygen in their blood?
What do you call a baby seal?
Which of these is the largest type of shark?
Which of these is NOT a fish?
How many arms does a typical starfish have?
A jellyfish's body is made up of roughly 95% of what?
Which sea creature is famous for being able to change colour to blend into its surroundings?
What is a group of fish swimming together usually called?
Clownfish make their home among the stinging tentacles of which animal?
A seahorse is unusual among animals because which parent carries the babies and gives birth?
What is coral mostly made of?
options: Colonies of tiny animals called polyps
Mineral deposits with no living part

prompt: Which fish can inflate itself into a spiky ball to scare off predators?
The 'Gulf Stream' is a famous example of what kind of ocean feature?
Sharks and rays have skeletons made of what, rather than bone?
Which of these whales is famous for the long, spiralled tusk that earned it the nickname 'unicorn of the sea'?
The sunlit surface layer of the ocean, where almost all photosynthesis happens, is known as which zone?
The mantis shrimp's club-like claw strikes so fast that it does what to the water around it?
options: Turns it briefly into a magnetic field
Splits it into pure hydrogen and oxygen
Boils it, creating bubbles that collapse with a flash of light
Freezes it into a thin shell of ice

prompt: Sea otters often hold hands or wrap themselves in kelp while sleeping. What is the main reason?
options: To keep from drifting apart on the current
To stay warm by sharing body heat
To hide their scent from sharks

prompt: Which of these is a real, well-documented behaviour of the mimic octopus?
options: Producing sound to communicate over long distances
Impersonating other animals like sea snakes and lionfish
Building shells from collected sand grains
Surviving by photosynthesis like a plant

prompt: Roughly what percentage of the world's oxygen is produced by ocean plankton and algae?
options: Almost none — forests make nearly all of it

prompt: Some sharks, like the great white, must keep swimming to breathe. What is this method of forcing water over the gills called?
